originalTitle: Nigeria to seek compensation for property abandoned by citizens fleeing South Africa
title: Nigeria Helps Friends Get Their Lost Things!
content:
Hello, My Little Friends!

Hello, hello, my wonderful friends! It's your friendly African news reporter here, ready to share some important news with you. Today, we're talking about how our big family in Africa helps each other. It's like when you help your friend find their lost toy, but on a much bigger scale! We're going to learn about how a country called Nigeria is helping its people.

What is This News About?

Imagine you have to leave your home very quickly. You might forget your favourite teddy bear or your shiny bicycle. That's a little bit like what happened to some people from Nigeria. They were living in a country called South Africa, but then they had to come back home. When they left, they couldn't bring all their things. Now, their home country, Nigeria, wants to help them get those things back, or get some money for them. It’s a story about a country looking after its family.

Our Nigerian Friends in South Africa

Long ago, many people from Nigeria went to live and work in South Africa. They built homes, opened small shops, and made new friends there. They had bakeries selling yummy bread, or tailor shops making beautiful clothes. They built houses where their families could live and play. These people were living their lives, just like you live in your homes now. They were part of the big family of Africa, just living in a different part. *Did you know?* Nigeria is a very big country in West Africa! It has lots of people and many different languages!

Why Did They Come Home?

Sometimes, things can get a little bit tricky and not so happy in places. For some of our Nigerian friends living in South Africa, things became difficult. It wasn't safe or easy for them to stay there anymore. So, they decided it was best to come back to their home country, Nigeria. We call people who come back home "returnees." It means they "returned" home. They had to leave South Africa and come back to Nigeria, their own country. It must have been a hard decision to make. *Did you know?* South Africa is at the very bottom of the continent of Africa. It's famous for its beautiful mountains and unique animals!

Leaving Things Behind

When you have to leave quickly, you can't always pack everything you own. Imagine leaving your bed, your kitchen table, or even your little shop where you sold tasty snacks! That's what happened. Many Nigerian people had to leave their houses, their cars, and their small businesses behind in South Africa. These are called "abandoned properties" or "abandoned businesses." It means they were left empty because the owners had to go. It must have felt sad to leave so many important things that you worked hard for.

Meet Our Special Helper: The High Commissioner!

Now, who is helping these people? It's a very important person called a "High Commissioner"! Imagine your school has a super special helper, someone who makes sure all the students from your class are okay, even if they are far away in another classroom. That's a bit like a High Commissioner! Nigeria has a special helper, a grown-up who is like a super ambassador for Nigeria in South Africa. This person's job is to look after Nigerian people who live in other countries. They are like a super friend from Nigeria, living far away to help our people. They listen to their problems and try to find solutions. This special helper is doing a very important job for Nigeria's people.

Making a List, Checking it Twice!

What is this High Commissioner doing? They are making a very important list! It's like when you make a list of all the toys you want for your birthday, but much more serious. They are writing down every house, every shop, every car, and every business that our Nigerian friends had to leave behind. This is called "documenting." It means writing down all the important details, like where the house was, what kind of shop it was, and who owned it. This list helps everyone remember exactly what was left and makes sure nothing is forgotten. It's a very careful and important job.

Asking for Help: Compensation

Once the list is finished, Nigeria wants to talk to South Africa. Nigeria will say, "Our friends left these important things behind. Can you help us get them back, or give them some money for what they lost?" This help, like getting money back for something you lost, is called "compensation." It means making things right again. It's like if someone accidentally broke your favourite crayon, and they bought you a new pack. That's a form of compensation! Nigeria wants to make sure its people are treated fairly and get what they deserve for their lost belongings. *Did you know?* The word "compensation" means to give something, usually money, to make up for a loss or damage!

Why This is Super Important

This news is super important because it shows that Nigeria really cares about its people. When your family needs help, you want to help them, right? It's the same for a country! Nigeria is showing that it will stand up for its citizens, even when they are far from home. It's about being fair and making sure everyone gets what they deserve. It teaches us about kindness, fairness, and looking out for each other, which are very important lessons for everyone, big and small. This helps people feel safe and cared for by their home country.

Helping Our African Family

Africa is a big continent, and all the countries in Africa are like one giant family. When one part of the family has a problem, other parts want to help. This story about Nigeria and South Africa shows us how countries can talk to each other to solve problems. It's about working together so everyone can live happily and safely. We are all connected, and helping each other makes our whole continent stronger and happier. It's a wonderful example of African countries working together. *Did you know?* Africa is the second-largest continent in the world! It has 54 countries and thousands of different cultures and languages.

Always Be Kind!

So, my little friends, remember this story. It teaches us about helping people who need it, about fairness, and about looking after each other. Whether it's your friend at school or people far away, being kind and standing up for what's right is always the best thing to do. Thank you for listening to our African news today! Keep smiling and keep being kind!
summary: Nigeria is asking South Africa for help to get back or pay for the houses and shops that Nigerian people had to leave behind when they returned home from South Africa.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows that Nigeria cares a lot about its people and wants to make sure they are treated fairly, helping them get back what they lost.

originalTitle: Sudan's RSF committed crimes against humanity in el-Fasher, Amnesty says
title: Helping Hands for Friends in Sudan: A Story of Safety and Hope
content:
Hello, my little explorers and curious listeners from all over Africa! I'm your friendly news reporter, here to share an important story with you today. It's about a place called Sudan, a big country in Africa, and a town there called el-Fasher. Sometimes, grown-ups have big problems, and it's good to talk about them simply so we can all understand and wish for peace.

What is Happening in El-Fasher?

Imagine your home, your school, and your favourite playground. They make you feel safe and happy, right? Well, in a town called el-Fasher in Sudan, some children and their families have been having a very difficult time. There has been fighting in their town, and when there is fighting, it can be very scary. When grown-ups fight, sometimes people get hurt, and many families have to leave their homes to find a safe place. This makes them feel sad and worried. It means children might not be able to go to school or play with their friends for a while. Did you know? Sudan is a very large country in Africa! It has a big river called the Nile running through it, and many different kinds of people live there. It's a land of sunshine and friendly faces, but sometimes, like in el-Fasher, things get tough.

Who Are the Helpers?

When bad things happen, there are always kind people and groups who try to help. One of these groups is called Amnesty International. Think of them like super helpers who travel around the world to make sure everyone is treated fairly and safely. They listen to people's stories and write reports about what is happening. Amnesty International looked closely at what happened in el-Fasher. They said that some very bad things were done to many people there. They found that a group of fighters, sometimes called the RSF, hurt people, took their things, and made them leave their homes. These actions are called 'crimes against humanity' – it means doing really, really wrong things to lots of innocent people. Did you know? The word 'humanity' means all of us, all the people in the world! So, 'crimes against humanity' means hurting all of us by hurting some of us.

Why is Being Safe So Important?

Every child, every grown-up, and every family deserves to feel safe. Safety means you can play, learn, sleep, and eat without worrying about bad things happening. When there is fighting, this feeling of safety goes away, and it makes everyone very sad and scared. Children in el-Fasher couldn't always go to school or play outside. They sometimes had to hide. Imagine not being able to kick a ball or read a story because it's not safe. That's why what Amnesty International does is so important – they shine a light on these problems so that people all over the world can know and try to help. Another big helper group is the United Nations, or UN for short. The UN is like a huge team of countries that work together to help people all over the world. They also looked at what happened in el-Fasher and said it was very serious. They want to make sure everyone is protected and that such sad things don't happen again. Did you know? The United Nations has many different groups that help children, like UNICEF! They work to make sure children have food, medicine, and schools, especially in places where things are difficult.

What Can We Do?

Even though we are far away, we can still wish for peace. We can learn about what is happening in the world and understand that everyone, everywhere, deserves kindness and safety. We can tell our grown-ups about what we've learned and ask how we can help others, even if it's just by being kind in our own neighbourhoods. The most important thing is for the fighting to stop in places like el-Fasher. When fighting stops, children can go back to school, families can go back to their homes, and everyone can start to feel safe and happy again. It's a big wish, but a very important one. We must remember that even when bad things happen, there are always people working to make things better. Amnesty International and the UN are two big helpers, and there are many more. They want to see peace and smiles return to the faces of the children and families in el-Fasher and all over Sudan. Let's all hope for peace and safety for our friends in Sudan. Let's remember to be kind to each other and help whenever we can, because everyone deserves a happy and safe place to live and grow. Until next time, stay curious and be kind, my little reporters!
summary: Amnesty International reported that very bad things happened to children and families in el-Fasher, Sudan, making them unsafe, and many groups like the UN are working to help bring peace and safety back to them.
whyMatters: It matters because all children and families, everywhere in Africa and the world, deserve to be safe, play, and learn without fear, and we should always hope for peace and help those who are hurting.

originalTitle: The Iran talks expose the collapse of US diplomacy
title: Let's Be Friends! How Countries Talk Together
content:
Hello, Amazing African Kids!

Hello, hello, my wonderful friends from all over Africa! I'm your friendly news reporter, and I'm so excited to tell you about something super important today. It's all about how countries, big and small, talk to each other. Just like you talk to your friends, countries also need to chat to make sure everyone is happy and safe. Isn't that cool? It's like having a giant playdate with all the countries in the world!

Why Do Countries Talk? (Like Friends!)

Imagine you are playing with your friends. Sometimes, you might want to share your toys, or maybe you have a small disagreement about whose turn it is. What do you do? You talk about it, right? You use your words to explain what you feel and to find a way to play nicely together. Countries do the exact same thing! Big countries and small countries all have things they want to share, like ideas for new medicines, or ways to grow more food. They also sometimes have problems they need to solve, like where a river flows or how to help people who are not well. Talking helps them sort everything out without getting upset. Did you know? There are nearly 200 different countries in the world! That's a lot of friends to talk to!

Who Helps Countries Talk?

Now, imagine you have a very big group of friends, and you all need to decide on a game. It might be a bit noisy and confusing, wouldn't it? That's why we have special people who help countries talk. These special people are called **diplomats**! Can you say that with me? Dip-low-mats! They are like super good listeners and problem-solvers. Diplomats are people who work for their country and go to other countries to talk. They listen carefully to what other countries need, and then they tell their own country's leaders. They try very, very hard to find solutions that make everyone happy. They are like the best playground monitors, making sure everyone plays fair! Did you know? Some diplomats can speak many different languages! This helps them talk to people from all over the world. How many languages can you speak?

What Makes a Good Talker?

What do you think makes a good friend to talk to? Someone who listens, right? And someone who is kind and fair. It's the same for countries! The people who talk for countries need to be very good at listening. They need to understand what the other country wants and why it's important to them. They also need to be good at explaining their own country's needs clearly and kindly. It's like when you try to explain to your friend why you want to play a certain game. You need to be clear and friendly. When countries talk, they try to be clear and friendly too, so they can become even better friends and help each other grow. Did you know? The United Nations is like a super big club where almost all the countries in the world come together to talk. They have meetings and try to solve big problems for everyone!

Sometimes Different People Talk

Sometimes, the person who helps countries talk might not be a regular diplomat. It might be someone else who is very good at understanding people and solving big problems. Maybe it's someone who built many things, or someone who is very wise. It's important that whoever is talking for a country is fair, kind, and wants to find the best way for everyone to live in peace. Just like when your teacher helps you and your friends solve a problem, they need to be fair to everyone, right? They don't pick sides; they just want everyone to be happy and learn. When different countries are talking, they want someone who can bring everyone together. They want someone who can make sure all countries feel heard and understood. It's like having a really good coach for a team – the coach helps everyone work together to win! Did you know? Sometimes countries sign special promises, like a very important pinky promise, to help each other. We call these promises 'treaties'!

Sharing and Caring Between Countries

When countries talk nicely and become friends, amazing things happen! They can share so much with each other. For example, one country might have lots of delicious fruits, and another country might have amazing new computers. Through talking, they can agree to share these things. This helps everyone get what they need and makes life better for all the people. It's like when you share your yummy snacks with your friend, and your friend shares their cool new toy with you. Everyone gets to enjoy more things! This sharing helps countries grow stronger and makes people's lives happier and healthier. Did you know? Africa is a giant continent with many, many different countries. All these countries often talk to each other to share ideas about farming, schooling, and building cool new things!

Building Bridges, Not Walls

Talking between countries is like building a bridge. A bridge helps people get from one side to another safely and easily. When countries talk, they build bridges of friendship and understanding. This helps them work together instead of being apart or fighting. It makes the whole world a safer and happier place for children like you to grow up in. When countries are friends, they can focus on important things like making sure everyone has enough food, clean water, and goes to school. Isn't that wonderful?

Your Turn to Talk!

So, my bright young friends, remember how important it is to talk! Talk to your family, talk to your friends, and listen to what they have to say. When we talk and listen, we learn, we share, and we make the world a better, kinder place for everyone. Keep shining, amazing African kids! Did you know? Even animals communicate with each other! Birds sing, elephants trumpet, and monkeys chatter to share messages. We all need to talk!
